name: Ait Ben Haddou — the UNESCO ksar

seo.title: Ait Ben Haddou — UNESCO ksar near Ouarzazate | Authentic Morocco Tours
seo.description: Visit the ksar of Ait Ben Haddou, a UNESCO World Heritage site and legendary film set near Ouarzazate. Private day trip from Marrakech or a stop on the desert route.

hero.tagline: The most famous ksar in southern Morocco, at the gates of the desert

intro: Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1987, the ksar of Ait Ben Haddou is the most impressive fortified earthen settlement in Morocco. Founded in the 11th century on the caravan route linking Sudan to Marrakech, its earthen houses with crenellated corner towers line the slope of a hill overlooking the Ounila Valley. Its spectacular silhouette has served as the backdrop for numerous international films and series — Lawrence of Arabia, Gladiator, Game of Thrones — making it one of the most photographed sites in North Africa. Located about 30 km from Ouarzazate and its film studios, it is an essential stop on the road linking Marrakech to the Merzouga desert, via the Tichka Pass and its High Atlas panoramas.

highlights.name: Earthen architecture
highlights.description: Fortified houses of raw earth with decorated towers, a remarkable example of pre-Saharan architecture, still partly inhabited.

highlights.name: Film backdrop
highlights.description: Location for numerous international productions — the Ouarzazate film studios are just minutes from the site.

highlights.name: View from the hilltop
highlights.description: The climb to the top of the ksar offers a full panorama over the Ounila Valley and the surrounding oasis.

highlights.name: Tichka Pass
highlights.description: The road from Marrakech crosses the High Atlas via this pass, at over 2,260 m altitude, with spectacular scenery.

practicalInfo.bestPeriod: Year-round, with a preference for spring and autumn for crossing the Tichka Pass
practicalInfo.daysRecommended: Day trip from Marrakech, or a stop on a multi-day tour towards the desert
tips: Morning and sunset light best highlights the site's ochre colours
Bring good shoes for the steep climb to the top of the ksar
Combine the visit with the Ouarzazate film studios, right nearby
